Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a sales budget within the Florencio Sales Manager application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

customerName.description: The name of the customer associated with the budget.

amount.description: The total monetary value of the budget.

status.description: Current status of the budget (e.g., New, Pending, Approved, Loss, Rejected).

rejectionReason.description: The reason for rejection, if the budget was rejected.

followUpDate.description: Date for a scheduled follow-up.  Can be used to track when the follow-up email/message was sent.

definition.description: Stores budget information for a specific user. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their budgets.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the budget.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage sales budgets within the Florencio Sales Manager application, prioritizing security, scalability, and ease of debugging. It emphasizes Authorization Independence by avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`) and ensures clear authorization intent. The structure also facilitates secure `list` operations and maintains data integrity.

Authorization Independence is achieved by avoiding nested data structures that would require `get()` calls in security rules. All data relevant to authorization for a given document is contained within that document itself or can be derived from the document's path (e.g., user ID from `/users/{userId}`).

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) are supported by ensuring that list operations can be performed securely without filtering data based on document content. Private user data is stored under path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/budgets/{budgetId}`). This ensures that only the user has access to their budgets, preventing unauthorized access through listing operations.

The structure uses structural segregation by placing budgets into a single collection under the user ID. This ensures that all budgets in a given collection share the same security posture, simplifying security rules.

Explicit State Modeling is implemented using the `status` field in the `Budget` entity, enabling clear tracking of the budget's lifecycle (New, Pending, Approved, Loss, Rejected).

The structure is designed for clarity and debuggability, with semantic naming conventions and standardized authorization fields.
